{"key_event":"1977 Civic Strike and 1978 Security Statute","reasoning":"Civil society became more mobilized through unions, students, ANUC, urban civic groups, and the 1977 strike, but gains were limited by National Front legacies, agrarian rollback, repression, guerrilla/criminal violence, and weak rural protections. State power rose slightly through executive dominance, state of siege, counterinsurgency successes, and the Security Statute, but was checked by legitimacy crises, guerrilla expansion, drug corruption, and incomplete territorial control.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":6.0,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":7.1}
